subcommands.name: --install
subcommands.description: Install a Windows Subsystem for Linux distribution

name: --distribution
-d
options.description: Specify the distribution to install

options.name: --enable-wsl1
options.description: Enable WSL1 support

options.name: --fixed-vhd
options.description: Create a fixed-size disk to store the distribution

name: --from-file
-f
options.description: Install a distribution from a local file

options.name: --legacy
options.description: Use the legacy distribution manifest

name: --location
-l
options.description: Set the install path for the distribution

options.name: --name
options.description: Set the name of the distribution

options.name: --no-distribution
options.description: Only install the required optional components, does not install a distribution

name: --no-launch
-n
options.description: Do not launch the distribution after install

options.name: --version
options.description: Specifies the version to use for the new distribution

options.name: --vhd-size
options.description: Specifies the size of the disk to store the distribution

options.name: --web-download
options.description: Download the distribution from the internet instead of the Microsoft Store

subcommands.name: --manage
subcommands.description: Changes distro specific options

name: --move
-m
options.description: Move the distribution to a new location

name: --set-sparse
-s
options.description: Set the VHD of distro to be sparse, allowing disk space to be automatically reclaimed

options.name: --set-default-user
options.description: Set the default user of the distribution

name: --resize
-r
options.description: Resize the disk of the distribution to the specified size

options.name: --allow-unsafe
options.description: Allow unsafe operations

subcommands.name: --mount
subcommands.description: Attaches and mounts a physical or virtual disk in all WSL 2 distributions

options.name: --vhd
options.description: Specifies that <Disk> refers to a virtual hard disk

name: --bare
-b
options.description: Attach the disk to WSL2, but don't mount it

name: --name
-n
options.description: Mount the disk using a custom name for the mountpoint

name: --type
-t
options.description: Filesystem to use when mounting a disk, if not specified defaults to ext4

name: --options
-o
options.description: Additional mount options

name: --partition
-p
options.description: Index of the partition to mount, if not specified defaults to the whole disk

subcommands.name: --unmount
subcommands.description: Unmounts and detaches a disk from all WSL2 distributions. Unmounts all disks if called without argument

subcommands.name: --export
subcommands.description: Exports the distribution to a tar file. The filename can be - for stdout

options.name: --vhd
options.description: Export as a VHD file

options.name: --format
options.description: Specifies the export format

subcommands.name: --import
subcommands.description: Imports the specified tar file as a new distribution. The filename can be - for stdin

options.name: --version
options.description: Specifies the version to use for the new distribution

options.name: --vhd
options.description: Specifies that the provided file is a .vhd or .vhdx file, not a tar file

subcommands.name: --import-in-place
subcommands.description: Imports the specified VHD file as a new distribution. The VHD must be formatted with the ext4 filesystem type

name: --list
-l
subcommands.description: Lists distributions

options.name: --all
options.description: List all distributions, including those currently being installed or uninstalled

options.name: --running
options.description: List only distributions that are currently running

name: --quiet
-q
options.description: Only show distribution names

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Show detailed information about all distributions

name: --online
-o
options.description: Displays a list of available distributions for install

name: --set-default
-s
--setdefault
subcommands.description: Sets the distribution as the default

subcommands.name: --set-version
subcommands.description: Changes the version of the specified distribution

subcommands.name: --set-default-version
subcommands.description: Changes the default install version for new distributions

name: --terminate
-t
subcommands.description: Terminates the specified distribution

subcommands.name: --unregister
subcommands.description: Unregisters the distribution and deletes the root filesystem

subcommands.name: --shutdown
subcommands.description: Immediately terminates all running distributions and the WSL 2 lightweight utility virtual machine

options.name: --force
options.description: Terminate the WSL 2 virtual machine even if an operation is in progress. Can cause data loss

subcommands.name: --update
subcommands.description: Update the Windows Subsystem for Linux package

options.name: --pre-release
options.description: Download a pre-release version if available

options.name: --web-download
options.description: Download from the internet instead of the Microsoft Store

subcommands.name: --status
subcommands.description: Show the status of Windows Subsystem for Linux

subcommands.name: --help
subcommands.description: Display usage information

name: --version
-v
subcommands.description: Display version information

subcommands.name: --uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstalls the Windows Subsystem for Linux package from this machine

subcommands.name: --debug-shell
subcommands.description: Open a WSL2 debug shell for diagnostics purposes

name: --distribution
-d
options.description: Run the specified distribution

options.name: --distribution-id
options.description: Run the specified distribution ID

name: --user
-u
options.description: Run as the specified user

name: --exec
-e
options.description: Execute the specified command without using the default Linux shell

options.name: --shell-type
options.description: Execute the specified command with the provided shell type

options.name: --cd
options.description: Sets the specified directory as the current working directory

options.name: --system
options.description: Launches a shell for the system distribution
